description_long: This object consists of two large, double-edged axe heads mounted on straight cylindrical handles, arranged in a crossed formation. The axe heads are broad, with a slightly curved cutting edge and a subtle taper towards the back, giving them a robust and functional appearance. The handles are smooth, straight, and appear to be made of a metallic material, with no visible grip enhancements or curvature. The surface of the axe heads shows a marbled, slightly weathered texture, suggesting use or intentional aging. The overall color palette is a mix of silvery gray tones, with some darker patches on the blades. The axes are symmetrical and lack any visible decorative markings or branding.
description: A pair of large, crossed double-edged axes with broad, curved blades and straight metallic handles in a silvery gray finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both axe heads fully, crossed handles, and the broad, curved blades.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the handles and the edge of the blades, with minimal blade width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying both axe heads and crossed handles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile of the handles and the edge of the blades.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical metallic handles with a smooth surface and no visible grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, curved double-edged blades with a slightly tapered back and no beard or poll; symmetrical and functional.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional, with the blade appearing to be fitted onto the handle via a central socket.
